overview: From 2026-05-23 to 2026-05-24, elizaos/eliza had 17 new PRs (2 merged), 0 new issues, and 8 active contributors.

topPRs.title: fix(core): preserve speaker names in prior dialogue context
topPRs.author: RemilioNubilio
topPRs.body:
## Summary - preserves connector-provided sender names when RECENT_MESSAGES history is rendered as structured `prior_message:user` blocks - keeps the existing provider-text dedupe behavior, so full `# Conversation Messages` provider text still stays out of Stage 1 prompts - adds a regression test for the Discord failure shape where a later turn asks about a named participant from earlier chat context ## Why A live Discord exchange showed Remilio/Nubilio losing speaker attribution after earlier chat context was converted into anonymous `prior_message:user` blocks. The model saw prior text, but not who said it, so it failed follow-up questions like "look in the chat" / "her and botdick" even though the relevant messages were present. This patch is connector-agnostic: it reads the existing `metadata.sender` / entity-name fields already written by connectors and prefixes prior dialogue content with the sender name. Reply references already use this shape. ## Validation - Synced branch to latest `origin/develop` (`fa83ddee6f`) via fast-forward before patching - `bun vitest run src/__tests__/message-runtime-stage1.test.ts src/features/basic-capabilities/providers/recentMessages.test.ts src/runtime/__tests__/planner-loop.test.ts` from `packages/core`: 94 tests passed - `bun run typecheck` from `packages/core`: passed - `bun run build` from `packages/core`: passed - Baseline after sync before patch: - core focused suite: 119 tests passed - orchestrator focused suite: 120 tests passed - Live Discord smoke after restart from local source: - setup message: `1507674411552084059` - prompt: `1507674422939750474` - bot reply: `1507674432225939507` - trajectory: `tj-224f2528525703` - model: `gpt-oss-120b` - tool calls: 0, failures: 0 - result: answered the shebotdick/botdick compatibility question using the recent channel context instead of claiming missing information

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes speaker-name attribution in Discord (and other connector) chat history by reading `metadata.sender` (and related fields) from each `Memory` object and prepending the name to prior-dialogue content blocks. It also adds a new response-handler evaluator that reroutes short chat-entity identity lookups through recall/memory instead of letting Stage 1 guess via the simple shortcut. - **Speaker preservation**: `priorDialogueSpeakerName` extracts the sender name from `memory.metadata.sender` (or a set of fallback fields) and `priorDialogueContent` prefixes the text with `"name: "`, deduplicated against an existing prefix. The `speakerName` is also stored in the segment metadata. - **Identity-lookup evaluator**: `extractContextualIdentityLookupSubject` parses the last non-empty message line, strips platform addressing, and routes matching patterns (e.g. "who is queuebot?") through `["general", "memory", "messaging"]` contexts with an `identity_lookup_policy` context slice; the existing simple-reply is cleared. - **Prompt hardening**: Both the DM handler template and the shared Stage 1 prompt gain a rule forbidding `replyText` that claims searches or recalls without an actual tool-call result this turn.

Confidence Score: 5/5

The core speaker-name fix is surgical and safe; the identity-lookup evaluator degrades gracefully on false positives rather than breaking. The speaker-attribution change touches only the prior-dialogue rendering path and is well-tested by the new regression test. The identity-lookup evaluator's heuristic can fire on common English words of 7+ lowercase characters (e.g. "yourself", "happening", "bitcoin"), but the worst-case outcome is a redundant planning cycle — `clearReply` is called, the model is given the `identity_lookup_policy` context slice, and it still produces a correct answer via the fallback policy. No data is corrupted and no response path is silently dropped. packages/core/src/services/message.ts — the `isShortContextualLookupSubject` heuristic in the new evaluator

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/core/src/services/message.ts | Adds speaker-name injection into prior_message:user blocks (core fix) and a new evaluator that reroutes contextual identity lookups through recall; the heuristic in `isShortContextualLookupSubject` over-matches common English words, causing unnecessary re-routing. | | packages/core/src/__tests__/message-runtime-stage1.test.ts | Adds four new test cases covering speaker-name preservation, identity-lookup routing, public-entity pass-through, and DM instruction text; all tests exercise the newly added code paths cleanly. | | packages/prompts/src/index.ts | Adds a single instruction line to the Stage 1 planner prompt forbidding hallucinated tool-call claims in `replyText`; change is narrow and correct. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[Incoming Memory / recentMessages] --> B[appendPriorDialogueEvents] B --> C[priorDialogueSpeakerName\nreads metadata.sender / fallback fields] C -->|name found| D[priorDialogueContent\nprepend 'name: ' to text] C -->|no name| E[emit text as-is] D --> F[prior_message:user segment\nwith speakerName in metadata] E --> F G[Stage 1 message handler result\ncontexts=simple, no tools] --> H{isSimpleMessageHandlerShortcut?} H -->|yes| I[extractContextualIdentityLookupSubject\nlastNonEmptyLine → strip platform addr → match prefix] I -->|subject found| J[core.contextual_identity_lookup_requires_recall evaluator] J --> K[setContexts: general, memory, messaging\nclearReply\naddContextSlices: identity_lookup_policy] K --> L[Planner receives context slice\nroutes through recall before answering] I -->|no match| M[pass through unchanged] H -->|no| M ``` Reviews (3): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(core,prompts): forbid ungrounded sim..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/3569ea21e08cebd62e6e6c862134c668dae432cc) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33576167)

topPRs.title: feat(cloud): JWT mint route + Steward plugin install (for Sol)
topPRs.author: 0xSolace
topPRs.body:
## Summary Ports the Sol/Steward Eliza Cloud bridge work from the accidental `0xSolace/milady-cloud` sibling repo into `elizaos/eliza` on `develop`. This is for Sol the Architect on waifu.fun: an Eliza Cloud agent should be able to receive a short-lived agent-native JWT, present it to Steward, and use the Steward Eliza plugin for policy-gated trading flows without long-lived static bearer secrets in the agent container. Changes included: - adds `POST /api/v1/agent-tokens` to mint short-lived RS256 Steward agent JWTs - extends the existing JWKS endpoint (also mounted at `/.well-known/jwks.json`) to publish the Steward agent-token public key alongside existing cloud JWKS keys - injects `STEWARD_JWT`, `STEWARD_JWT_FILE`, `STEWARD_REFRESH_URL`, `STEWARD_AGENT_ID`, and `STEWARD_API_URL` into Docker-provisioned agent containers when an agent-token signing key is configured - starts a lightweight in-container refresh sidecar when `ELIZA_CLOUD_SERVICE_TOKEN` / `AGENT_TOKEN_SERVICE_TOKEN` is configured - auto-installs `@stwd/eliza-plugin` for `agentId === "sol"` or when `STEWARD_ENABLE_TRADE_PLUGIN=true` ## Operator env - `AGENT_TOKEN_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM` or `ELIZA_AGENT_TOKEN_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM` — RS256 private key for Steward agent JWTs - `AGENT_TOKEN_KEY_ID` or `ELIZA_AGENT_TOKEN_KEY_ID` — optional `kid`, otherwise derived from the public JWK thumbprint - `ELIZA_CLOUD_SERVICE_TOKEN` or `AGENT_TOKEN_SERVICE_TOKEN` — service token used by the refresh sidecar and `/api/v1/agent-tokens` - `STEWARD_REFRESH_URL` — optional override; defaults from the Cloud API public URL - `STEWARD_ELIZA_PLUGIN_PACKAGE` — optional plugin package override; defaults to `@stwd/eliza-plugin` - `STEWARD_ENABLE_TRADE_PLUGIN=true` — optional rollout flag for non-`sol` test agents ## Notes This PR is intentionally draft for Shadow/Shaw review before un-drafting. The original Sprint 4 architecture notes live in Sol's project workspace; I did not link the private file directly here. ## Verification - `bunx biome check --write` on changed files - agent-token smoke with a generated RS256 key: minted `agent_id=sol` JWT and emitted JWKS with `alg=RS256` - `bun run --cwd packages/cloud-api typecheck` currently fails on pre-existing workspace resolution/generated-file issues unrelated to this change (`@elizaos/plugin-elizacloud`, `@elizaos/contracts`, `validation-keyword-data`); no remaining type errors referenced the new agent-token files after fixing `JWK.oth` typing.

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `POST /api/v1/agent-tokens` endpoint for minting short-lived RS256 Steward JWTs, extends the JWKS endpoint to publish the corresponding public key, and wires Docker-provisioned containers to receive the initial JWT plus a background refresh sidecar. - **New JWT mint route** (`v1/agent-tokens/route.ts` + `cloud-shared/.../agent-token.ts`): signs RS256 JWTs for named agents using an operator-provided PEM key, with in-module caching and a JWKS export helper. - **Docker container integration** (`docker-sandbox-provider.ts`): mints an initial JWT at provision time, injects it as the `STEWARD_JWT` env var, optionally starts a `docker exec -d` refresh sidecar every 600 s, and conditionally runs `npm install @stwd/eliza-plugin` in the running container for the `sol` agent or when `STEWARD_ENABLE_TRADE_PLUGIN=true`. - **Auth middleware** (`middleware/auth.ts`): adds `/api/v1/agent-tokens` to the public-path list, delegating all auth to the route's own service-token / admin check.

Confidence Score: 2/5

Not ready to merge: the Docker container provisioning path injects a live Steward trading credential as a plain env var, the service-token comparison on the JWT mint endpoint is not constant-time, and an operator misconfiguration silently leaves containers with a permanently-expired JWT after 15 minutes. Three distinct issues directly affect security or correctness on the new code paths: a timing-oracle on the only credential guarding JWT minting, a short-lived trading JWT exposed through Docker metadata, and a deployment scenario where token refresh is structurally impossible. docker-sandbox-provider.ts (JWT env injection, refresh sidecar guard, plugin install ordering) and v1/agent-tokens/route.ts (service-token comparison) need the most attention before merge.

Security Review

 - **Timing-unsafe token comparison** (`v1/agent-tokens/route.ts:41`): `supplied === expected` for the service-account token is not constant-time; an attacker who can measure response latency can enumerate the token and gain the ability to mint arbitrary Steward JWTs. - **JWT in Docker environment variable** (`docker-sandbox-provider.ts:641`): `STEWARD_JWT` is placed in the container environment, exposing a live Steward credential via `docker inspect` and any log sink that dumps env vars. - **Private key export in JWKS derivation** (`agent-token.ts:83`): the private `CryptoKey` is exported to a full JWK and private fields are stripped by name; any unlisted private parameter would silently appear in the public JWKS response.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/docker-sandbox-provider.ts | Adds JWT injection via Docker env var (exposure risk), plugin install after container start (won't take effect), refresh sidecar that silently expires when service token is absent, and brittle sed-based JSON parsing | | packages/cloud-api/v1/agent-tokens/route.ts | New route for minting Steward RS256 JWTs; auth logic bypasses global middleware and uses a timing-unsafe string comparison for the service token | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/auth/agent-token.ts | New JWT signing module; fragile private-key-export-then-strip pattern for public JWK derivation; caching logic is correct | | packages/cloud-api/src/middleware/auth.ts | Adds /api/v1/agent-tokens to public path prefixes so the route handles its own auth; intentional but worth noting as the endpoint mints privileged credentials | | packages/cloud-api/.well-known/jwks.json/route.ts | Extends JWKS endpoint to merge agent-token public key alongside existing cloud keys; logic is clean and cache headers are preserved | | packages/cloud-api/src/bootstrap-app.ts | Mounts the JWKS route at /.well-known/jwks.json before the auth middleware; straightforward change | | .gitleaks.toml | Adds path-scoped allowlist for PEM envelope template wrappers in agent-token.ts; appropriate | | .gitleaksignore | New fingerprint ignore for the original commit where PEM markers appeared unsplit; explained in comment as a CI scan range false positive |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Provisioner as DockerSandboxProvider participant CloudAPI as Cloud API participant JWKS as /.well-known/jwks.json participant Container as Docker Container participant Sidecar as Refresh Sidecar participant Steward as Steward Provisioner->>CloudAPI: "mintAgentToken(agentId, ttl=900)" CloudAPI-->>Provisioner: token + expiresAt Provisioner->>Container: "docker create -e STEWARD_JWT=token" Provisioner->>Container: docker start Provisioner->>Container: "npm install @stwd/eliza-plugin" Provisioner->>Sidecar: docker exec -d sh refresh-loop.sh loop Every 600s Sidecar->>CloudAPI: POST /api/v1/agent-tokens CloudAPI-->>Sidecar: new token Sidecar->>Container: write token to STEWARD_JWT_FILE end Container->>Steward: request with JWT Steward->>JWKS: GET /.well-known/jwks.json JWKS-->>Steward: keys including agentTokenPublicKey ``` Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(cloud): silence gitleaks false posit..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/287463efa54e21f546b501ef3a68bfb37e2f94e6) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33597774) > Greptile also left **6 inline comments** on this PR.

topPRs.title: docs(os-release): add VERIFY.md + verify-release.sh helper
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary Ships a layered release verification helper that complements the in-progress signing / attestation / SBOM work: - `packages/os/release/VERIFY.md` — user-facing verification doc - `packages/os/scripts/verify-release.sh` — one-command runner The script walks four layers in order against a downloaded release dir: 1. `SHA256SUMS` roundtrip — required (coreutils only) 2. GitHub artifact attestations — optional (gh CLI) 3. GPG signature on `SHA256SUMS` — optional (gpg) 4. SBOM summary — optional (jq) Each optional layer is **skipped with a notice** when its tool is missing, so the script is useful out of the box on any Unix. ## Exit codes | Code | Meaning | | --- | --- | | `0` | every required check passed (optional layers may have been skipped or warned) | | `1` | SHA256SUMS missing or roundtrip failed | | `2` | an optional layer detected real corruption / tampering (mixed valid+invalid attestations, or a bad GPG sig). Pure absence is NOT treated as tampering. | ## Validation - `shellcheck packages/os/scripts/verify-release.sh` → clean - 5-case test matrix: 1. **clean dir** → exit 0 (SHA256SUMS OK; attestations not yet rolled out so warned, not failed) 2. **corrupted artifact** → exit 1 with SHA256SUMS `FAILED` line 3. **missing SHA256SUMS** → exit 1 4. **bad GPG sig file** → exit 2 with `gpg --verify` failure printed 5. **SHA256SUMS with header comments** (mirrors the format produced by the in-repo `generate-release-checksums.mjs` script) → exit 0; comment lines correctly tolerated by `sha256sum -c` ## Open questions - The script defaults to `--owner elizaOS` for `gh attestation verify`. If the org name moves, that's a one-line change. - The "no attestations verified" case is treated as a warning, not a failure, so users running this against pre-attestation releases get a clean exit. Once attestation rollout is universal, this could be tightened to fail-closed. - VERIFY.md references the future `SHA256SUMS.asc` GPG flow. If the signing-key RFC lands a different path (e.g. cosign-only), the doc needs a minor edit.

Greptile Summary

This PR ships two new files that add a layered release-verification helper for elizaOS OS packages: a user-facing `VERIFY.md` guide and a `verify-release.sh` script that walks SHA256SUMS integrity, GitHub artifact attestations, GPG signature, and SBOM inspection in order. - **`verify-release.sh`** — drives all four layers, uses `EXIT` accumulation so optional-layer failures don't short-circuit the report, and exits 0/1/2 based on required vs. optional layer outcomes. - **`VERIFY.md`** — documents each layer with manual commands and maps exit codes; references a future GPG signing-key RFC and future SBOM/attestation rollout.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Safe to land as documentation/tooling, but logic gaps in the attestation loop and GPG check mean the script can report a clean result when no artifacts were actually verified. The attestation loop silently produces '0 valid, 0 not verified' with no warning when all referenced artifact files are absent from disk. Combined with the already-flagged --ignore-missing Layer 1 pass, a directory containing only SHA256SUMS and no artifacts receives a clean report from both layers without a single file being checked. The GPG check also uses locale-dependent grep instead of the gpg exit code. packages/os/scripts/verify-release.sh — the attestation result classification logic (lines 78–83) and the GPG check (line 95) both need attention before the script can be relied upon for release verification.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/os/scripts/verify-release.sh | New 4-layer verification script; attestation loop has a silent 0/0 edge case (no files on disk), the GPG check uses locale-dependent grep rather than gpg exit code, and Layer 1 passes with --ignore-missing when no artifacts are present. | | packages/os/release/VERIFY.md | New user-facing verification guide; Layer 1 description claims sha256sum --ignore-missing works on any Unix (GNU coreutils only), and Layer 2 manual example includes SHA256SUMS itself which the automated script does not attest. | Reviews (2): Last reviewed commit: ["docs(os-release): add VERIFY.md + verify..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/f97d565c1ea48966c47b2de38122c47a76a27188) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33614352) > Greptile also left **1 inline comment** on this PR.

topPRs.title: fix(cloud-api): KMS local-backend with persistent root key for prod (P0)
topPRs.author: 0xSolace
topPRs.body:
## What P0 production incident: every SIWE signup on api.elizacloud.ai is failing with HTTP 500 and an internal `KmsError`. Confirmed via `wrangler tail` on `eliza-cloud-api-prod`. ## Root cause 1. `apiKeysService.create()` encrypts the new api_key via `encryptField()` 2. `getKmsClient()` lazily calls `createKmsClient()` (no args) 3. `resolveKmsBackend()` returns `steward` (production default) 4. The `steward` branch needs `steward.{baseUrl, tokenProvider}` config — nobody passes it 5. Throws `KmsError` → caught by Hono error middleware → 500 Additionally, `StewardKmsAdapter` is currently a stub: every method throws `NotImplementedError("Steward endpoint not yet available")`. So even with proper config, the steward path fails. ## Fix - `packages/security/src/kms/index.ts`: - Allow the factory to read an explicit `env` source for Cloudflare Workers (where `process.env` is empty) - When local backend is selected, source the root key from `ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY` (base64-encoded 32 bytes), falling back to random only under `NODE_ENV=test` - Throw a clear error when local backend is selected in production without a root key (instead of silently generating a random one that would lose every ciphertext across cold starts) - Graceful fallback: if `steward` is selected but `opts.steward` is missing AND `ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY` is set, fall back to local with a console warning (lets us un-stick prod without changing the env-default) - `packages/cloud-shared/src/db/crypto/kms-client.ts`: - Pass `getCloudAwareEnv()` to `createKmsClient()` so Workers see the env vars - `packages/security/src/__tests__/factory.test.ts`: - 18 new tests covering: backend resolution, env-key parsing, fallback rules, error cases ## Verification - `bun test` in `packages/security` → 28/28 pass ## Operator action required after merge On `eliza-cloud-api-prod` (and staging): ``` ELIZA_KMS_BACKEND=local ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 32) # 32-byte secret ``` Co-authored-by: wakesync

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a production outage on Eliza Cloud where every SIWE signup was failing with HTTP 500 because `createKmsClient()` defaulted to the `steward` backend (an unimplemented stub) and could not find required steward configuration. The fix routes the cloud API to the `local` AEAD backend keyed from `ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY`, with a graceful steward→local fallback, and passes Cloudflare Worker secrets through `getCloudAwareEnv()` so they are visible to the factory. - **`packages/security/src/kms/index.ts`**: Factory now reads `opts.env` (or `process.env`) for backend selection and root-key decoding; adds `decodeRootKey` + `resolveLocalRootKey` helpers, a hard error when no persistent key is available outside test environments, and a steward→local fallback when `ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY` is set but no steward config is provided. - **`packages/cloud-shared/src/db/crypto/kms-client.ts`**: Passes `getCloudAwareEnv()` into `createKmsClient` so the Cloudflare Worker secret store is merged with `process.env` during factory init; adds `resetKmsClientForTests()` for test isolation. - **`packages/security/src/__tests__/factory.test.ts`**: 18 new tests covering backend resolution, env-key parsing, fallback logic, and error cases.

Confidence Score: 3/5

The cloud-API path and new tests look correct, but the factory change quietly breaks any existing deployment using ELIZA_LOCAL_MODE=1 without a persistent root key. The KMS factory now throws a hard error for the ELIZA_LOCAL_MODE=1 desktop path whenever ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY is absent and NODE_ENV is not test. Before this change those deployments would get an ephemeral random key — lossy but non-fatal; after the change they fail to start. That behavioral shift is undocumented and affects a real deployment class. The fix for the production cloud incident is sound, and env-forwarding through getCloudAwareEnv is correct, but the desktop breakage is a present defect on the changed path. packages/security/src/kms/index.ts — the resolveLocalRootKey change that now throws for ELIZA_LOCAL_MODE=1 deployments without a root key, and the dead try/catch in decodeRootKey

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/security/src/kms/index.ts | Core factory rewrite: adds env-aware backend resolution, persistent root-key decoding, steward→local fallback, and a hard error when no root key is set outside test — introduces a breaking behavior change for ELIZA_LOCAL_MODE=1 desktop deployments, and contains a dead try/catch around Buffer.from | | packages/cloud-shared/src/db/crypto/kms-client.ts | Passes getCloudAwareEnv() to createKmsClient so Workers see secrets from c.env; adds resetKmsClientForTests(); change is minimal and correct | | packages/security/src/__tests__/factory.test.ts | 18 new vitest cases covering backend resolution, env-key parsing, steward fallback, and error paths; missing a case for ELIZA_LOCAL_MODE=1 without a root key in a non-test env |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[getKmsClient] --> B{_kms set?} B -- yes --> Z[return _kms] B -- no --> C[getCloudAwareEnv\nALS store ∪ process.env] C --> D[createKmsClient opts.env] D --> E[resolveKmsBackend] E --> F{opts.backend?} F -- yes --> G[use explicit backend] F -- no --> H{ELIZA_KMS_BACKEND env?} H -- memory/local/steward --> G H -- unrecognized or absent --> I{NODE_ENV=test?} I -- yes --> J[memory] I -- no --> K{ELIZA_LOCAL_MODE=1?} K -- yes --> L[local] K -- no --> M[steward default] G --> N{backend = steward and !opts.steward?} M --> N N -- ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY set --> O[warn + override to local] N -- no local key --> P[throw KmsError] O --> Q[resolveLocalRootKey] L --> Q Q --> R{opts.local.rootKey?} R -- yes --> S[use explicit key] R -- no --> T{ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY env?} T -- set --> U[decodeRootKey base64] T -- absent, test env --> V[randomRootKey ephemeral] T -- absent, non-test --> W[throw KmsError] S --> X[new LocalKmsAdapter] U --> X V --> X J --> Y[new MemoryKmsAdapter] X --> Z Y --> Z ``` Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(cloud-api): KMS local-backend with p..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/9466e44baf579c191586eff56e0e245d994b4e1e) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33618009) > Greptile also left **2 inline comments** on this PR.

topPRs.title: feat(cloud-shared): rebrand-ready agent base domain config (waifu.fun → elizacloud.ai prep)
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
## Summary Prepare the cloud-shared layer for the rebrand from `waifu.fun` / `milady.ai` / `shad0w.xyz` to `elizacloud.ai`. **Runtime impact: zero** until `ELIZA_CLOUD_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN` flips in deployment env. This PR is one of several coordinated commits that ship the rebrand without breaking existing sandbox URLs. ## Changes 1. **`DOMAIN_ALIAS_GROUPS`** extended from the original `[waifu.fun, eliza.ai]` pair to the full 5-domain group: ```ts [".waifu.fun", ".eliza.ai", ".elizacloud.ai", ".milady.ai", ".shad0w.xyz"] ``` The pairing-token CORS Origin validator now tries every alternate, so a token issued against `.waifu.fun` validates when the user reaches `.elizacloud.ai` (or any other group member). Without this, the rebrand would break in-flight pair handshakes. 2. **Pure module extraction**: `getAlternateDomainOrigins()` + the `DOMAIN_ALIAS_GROUPS` constant move into a new file `pairing-token-domains.ts` so the alias logic is unit-testable without dragging the Postgres repository import chain. The DB-bound `PairingTokenService` in `pairing-token.ts` re-exports the same names for back-compat with existing callers. 3. **`DEFAULT_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN`** in `eliza-agent-web-ui.ts` flips from `"waifu.fun"` to `"elizacloud.ai"`. A fresh deploy without `ELIZA_CLOUD_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN` set picks the rebrand brand by default. Existing deployments override via env var (currently `"milady.ai"` on the prod manager VM) and stay unchanged. 4. **Stale comment fix** in `eliza-sandbox.ts:2190`: replaced `// Public URL: https://{agentId}.waifu.fun/...` with the env-var-templated equivalent so the comment ages with the deployment. ## Tests 8 sociable specs in `pairing-token.test.ts`: - Every domain in the group resolves to the 4 others - Suffix anchoring (`abc.notwaifu.fun` does NOT alias) - UUID prefix preservation across rewrites - Port round-trip (https://abc.waifu.fun:8443 → https://abc.eliza.ai:8443) - Unparseable input returns `[]` - `DOMAIN_ALIAS_GROUPS` declares `.elizacloud.ai` (the rebrand-target guarantee) - Suffixes are leading-dot anchored `bun test packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/pairing-token.test.ts` → **8/8 pass**. ## What this PR does NOT do - Set `ELIZA_CLOUD_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN=elizacloud.ai` in deployed env — that's an ops action, separate from code - Create new DNS records or Cloudflare tunnel ingress entries — also ops - Touch the existing 22 `agent_sandboxes` rows (their `bridge_url` uses direct IPs, not affected by DNS) - Drop `.waifu.fun` / `.milady.ai` / `.shad0w.xyz` from the alias group — they stay during the grace period so existing pair tokens validate ## Verification - [x] `bun test packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/pairing-token.test.ts` - [x] `bunx tsc --noEmit` on `packages/cloud-shared` (clean, only pre-existing core/shared @elizaos/contracts noise) - [x] `bunx @biomejs/biome check` on touched files (clean) - [ ] After merge: flip `ELIZA_CLOUD_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN=elizacloud.ai` on the prod manager VM `.env.local` and restart the daemons (ops, not in this PR)

Greptile Summary

This PR prepares the `cloud-shared` package for the `elizacloud.ai` rebrand by extending the pairing-token domain alias group from 2 to 3 entries (adding `.elizacloud.ai`), flipping `DEFAULT_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN`, and extracting the alias logic into a standalone, unit-testable module. Runtime impact is zero until the env var is flipped in deployment. - **`pairing-token-domains.ts`** (new file): extracts `DOMAIN_ALIAS_GROUPS` and `getAlternateDomainOrigins` out of the DB-coupled `PairingTokenService`; notably replaces the old regex-based suffix swap with clean `string.slice` + concatenation, which incidentally resolves the previously raised regex-metachar escaping concern. - **`pairing-token.ts`**: replaces the old single-alias `getAlternateDomainOrigin` private method with a loop over `getAlternateDomainOrigins`, breaking as soon as a valid token row is found; note that `await hashToken(token)` is recomputed on every loop iteration (flagged in a prior review). - **`eliza-agent-web-ui.ts`** / **`eliza-sandbox.ts`**: comment-only and default-value updates; no logic change. **Note on PR description accuracy:** The description states `DOMAIN_ALIAS_GROUPS` contains 5 domains including `.milady.ai` and `.shad0w.xyz`, but the actual code contains 3. The code comment and a dedicated test case (`"rejects retired 0xSolace-era domains"`) explicitly document this as intentional — those two domains are excluded because they never served agent-sandbox traffic. The description is misleading but the code and tests are internally consistent.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — all logic changes are guarded by an env-var override, so the default domain flip is a no-op for any deployment that already sets ELIZA_CLOUD_AGENT_BASE_DOMAIN. The alias-group extension and module extraction are straightforward and well-tested. The new suffix-slice approach is cleaner than the replaced regex path. No existing behaviour changes until the ops env-var is flipped, and the test suite explicitly guards every boundary condition including the excluded legacy domains. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/pairing-token-domains.ts | New module extracting domain alias logic; uses safe string-slice suffix replacement instead of the previous regex approach; well-commented with intentional exclusion of milady.ai and shad0w.xyz. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/pairing-token.ts | Replaces private single-alias method with a loop over getAlternateDomainOrigins; hashToken is still recomputed per iteration (flagged in a prior review comment). | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/pairing-token.test.ts | New test file with 8 targeted specs covering suffix anchoring, UUID prefix preservation, port round-trips, excluded domains, and edge-case inputs. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/eliza-agent-web-ui.ts | Default domain flipped from waifu.fun to elizacloud.ai; jsdoc comment updated to match; no logic change. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts | Single comment line updated to reference the env-var template instead of a hardcoded waifu.fun URL; no logic change. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[validateToken called] --> B{expectedOrigin parseable?} B -- No --> Z[return null] B -- Yes --> C[normalizedOrigin = new URL origin] C --> D[consumeValidToken hash+normalizedOrigin] D --> E{row found?} E -- Yes --> R[return PairingToken] E -- No --> F[getAlternateDomainOrigins] F --> G{hostname matches DOMAIN_ALIAS_GROUPS?} G -- No match --> Z2[return null] G -- Matched --> H[build alternates list] H --> I[loop over alternates] I --> J[consumeValidToken hash+alternateOrigin] J --> K{row found?} K -- Yes --> R K -- No, more --> I K -- No more --> Z2 ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/pairing-token.ts`, line 89-108 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/674ad400ca1be1388e7909832298f14da4c8c0ae/packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/pairing-token.ts#L89-L108)) `hashToken` is an async SHA-256 digest, and the result is identical on every call for the same `token`. Currently it is awaited once for the exact-origin check and then re-awaited on every iteration of the alternates loop (up to 4 more times). Computing it once and reusing the result avoids redundant crypto work on every miss.
Reviews (3): Last reviewed commit: ["Merge branch 'develop' into feat/cloud-r..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/5166db6ffcc84b33504e8955403e715510b35d6a) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33576472)
